title: Hand-rolled JSON extractor: defend against 'just use serde_json'
description: serde_json::from_str deserializes all field values into heap-allocated Strings before you can access any one field. For a JSON blob like {"password":"s3cr3t","api_key":"abc123"}, that means 'api_key' lands in heap memory even if you only want 'password'. Those bytes are not zeroized on drop. The hand-rolled scanner allocates only the target field's value. A contributor who suggests 'this is reinventing the wheel, use serde_json' should be told: the entire point is that serde_json would leave other secrets in unzeroized memory. This is the core design invariant of the library, not an accident.
